---
title: "Word_Template"
author: "Deon Roos"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  word_document:
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 3
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# R Markdown

asldkjnasld

## Topic 2

askdsakdn `blah()`

### Topic 3

aspdjasidj

# oiAJHS

> Quotation area

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>. or [here](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com)

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

# Lists

* abs

* asodiasd

+ askdm

+ aspidj

- paksdsa

- aksmdsakd

1. apskdmsa

2. lkaspljmdkopas

3. paosdsad

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
# comment
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.


```{r, eval = F}
# Comments are not read as code.
# They are notes for ourselves and others.
my_result <- a_function(argument1 = "character1", 
                        argument2 = variableA,
                        argument3 = 1234,
                        argument4 = c(TRUE, FALSE),
                        argument5 = my_data)
```
